title: St. John's Church, Meerut
tagline: St. John's Church, Meerut — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

heroImage.alt: St. John's Church, Meerut, Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: The Saint John the Baptist, or John's Church, is a parish church in the diocese of Agra of Church of North India, and is located Meerut, India.
overview.description: The Saint John the Baptist, or John's Church, is a parish church in the diocese of Agra of Church of North India, and is located Meerut, India. The church building was built from 1819 to 1822, and is the oldest "Garrison Church" in North India. Near to this church lies St. John's Church cemetery.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: MES Colony metro station
topPlaces.description: The MES Colony metro station is an elevated Meerut Metro station on the Delhi–Ghaziabad–Meerut RRTS corridor (also known as the Namo Bharat corridor) serving Lekha Nagar in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, India. It serves as a halt for Meerut Metro trains that run at a speed of 120 km/h.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Academy, Meerut
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Academy is a private, college preparatory school for day students located in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, India in the NCR Region.

topPlaces.name: Diocese of Meerut
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Meerut (Latin: Meeruten(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Meerut in the ecclesiastical province of Agra in India.

topPlaces.name: Meerut division
topPlaces.description: Meerut division (Hindi pronunciation: [meːɾəʈʰ]) is one of the 18 administrative geographical units (i.e. division) of the northern Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: 2006 Meerut fire
topPlaces.description: On 10 April 2006 at about 17:30 IST (12:00 UTC), a fire swept through a consumer electronics fair (Brand India Fair) in Victoria Park, Meerut, killing 65 people and injuring 150 others. The fire was said to have been caused by a short circuit.

topPlaces.name: Bhoopgarhi
topPlaces.description: Bhoopgarhi is a small village in the Meerut district of Uttar Pradesh, near the Vikas Khand administrative region of Jani Khurd, on the Bagpat road. The village was established in 1910 by Chaudhary Bhoopat Singh Teotia, who came from Bhatona village in Bulandshahar, West Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Khatik Mohalla (Meerut)
topPlaces.description: Khatik Mohalla is a town in Meerut district of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is located 6.2 kilometres from Meerut bus stand, 6.3 kilometres from Meerut,7.3 kilometres from Meerut cantt railway station and 4.6 kilometres from Army Hospital, Meerut Cantt.

topPlaces.name: Meerut
topPlaces.description: Meerut is a city that serves as the administrative headquarters of the Meerut district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The city lies in western Uttar Pradesh and is part of the Indian National Capital Region (NCR).

items.note: The MES Colony metro station is an elevated Meerut Metro station on the Delhi–Ghaziabad–Meerut RRTS corridor (also known as the Namo Bharat corridor) serving Lekha Nagar in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, India. It serves as a halt for Meerut Metro trains that run at a speed of 120 km/h.
St. Mary's Academy is a private, college preparatory school for day students located in Meerut, Uttar Pradesh, India in the NCR Region.
The Roman Catholic Diocese of Meerut (Latin: Meeruten(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Meerut in the ecclesiastical province of Agra in India.
Meerut division (Hindi pronunciation: [meːɾəʈʰ]) is one of the 18 administrative geographical units (i.e. division) of the northern Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

On 10 April 2006 at about 17:30 IST (12:00 UTC), a fire swept through a consumer electronics fair (Brand India Fair) in Victoria Park, Meerut, killing 65 people and injuring 150 others. The fire was said to have been caused by a short circuit.

Bhoopgarhi is a small village in the Meerut district of Uttar Pradesh, near the Vikas Khand administrative region of Jani Khurd, on the Bagpat road. The village was established in 1910 by Chaudhary Bhoopat Singh Teotia, who came from Bhatona village in Bulandshahar, West Uttar Pradesh.

Khatik Mohalla is a town in Meerut district of Uttar Pradesh, India. It is located 6.2 kilometres from Meerut bus stand, 6.3 kilometres from Meerut,7.3 kilometres from Meerut cantt railway station and 4.6 kilometres from Army Hospital, Meerut Cantt.

Meerut is a city that serves as the administrative headquarters of the Meerut district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. The city lies in western Uttar Pradesh and is part of the Indian National Capital Region (NCR).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. John's Church, Meerut?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. John's Church, Meerut is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. John's Church, Meerut?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Indira Gandhi International Airport, Delhi (78 km) and the nearest railway station is New Delhi Railway Station (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. John's Church, Meerut?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. John's Church, Meerut at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. John's Church, Meerut?
faq.a: Stays in St. John's Church, Meerut start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. John's Church, Meerut?
faq.a: The top attractions are MES Colony metro station, St. Mary's Academy, Meerut, Diocese of Meerut, Meerut division, 2006 Meerut fire.
